scope: CapSched-H production assurance case with Linux-only L0 prototype boundaries

status_legend.open: Required for the final goal but not established.
status_legend.model_supported: Supported by small or decomposed semantic models.
status_legend.prototype_evidenced: Supported by Linux compatibility or integration evidence only.
status_legend.protection_evidenced: Supported by monitor-backed production implementation and validation.
status_legend.forbidden_for_l0: Must not be claimed by Linux-only prototypes.

claims.title: Cross-Domain escape requires monitor or typed endpoint break
claims.claim: A Domain-local userspace and Linux-kernel-context compromise cannot cross into another Domain's memory, execution authority, device queue, DMA memory, or control authority without breaking the HyperTag Monitor or an explicitly exposed typed service endpoint.
gaps: No per-Domain mutable kernel state implementation.

claims.title: Non-forgeable Domain activation
claims.claim: Linux-visible Domain shadows do not create active authority; monitor validation of RunToken, epoch, placement, root budget, and MemoryView is required.
gaps: No proof that Linux cannot mutate active roots.

claims.title: No CPU execution without runnable authority
claims.claim: A task cannot enter or remain executable without a live RunCap-derived FrozenRunUse, SchedContext, epoch, generation, CPU placement, and remaining budget.

claims.title: CPU and service execution stay within root budget
claims.claim: Domain CPU use and service execution on behalf of callers are bounded by non-forgeable root budgets and caller-reserved BudgetTickets.

claims.title: Resource access requires typed endpoint authority
claims.claim: Resource operations require EndpointCap-derived frozen use; scheduler RunCap is not resource authority.

claims.title: Async work preserves caller provenance
claims.claim: Domain-derived async work executes with caller provenance and frozen caller authority.
gaps: No timer, RCU, softirq, or completion provenance policy.

claims.title: Other Domain memory and mutable kernel state are unmapped
claims.claim: Domain-private user pages and mutable kernel pages are mapped only in the owning MemoryView or through explicit service/shared buffers.
gaps: No per-Domain mutable kernel-state allocator plan.

claims.title: No stale translation crosses activation or revoke
claims.claim: Domain activation and page revoke leave no usable stale direct-map, stage-2/EPT, ASID/PCID/VMID, IOMMU, or CPU TLB translation into another Domain.

claims.title: Mutable page cache is Domain-owned or service-mediated
claims.claim: Sealed base content may be shared, but mutable overlays are Domain-owned and commits are serialized through a service endpoint or equivalent token.

claims.title: Queue submit, DMA, IRQ, and rate revoke as one lease
claims.claim: Device queue ownership, allowed DMA memory, interrupt route, epoch, and queue budget/rate limit are one monitor-owned QueueLease boundary.
gaps: No monitor-backed QueueTag, QueueControlCap, RepresentorForwardCap, typed SubmitLedger, or DescriptorLedger.

claims.title: Modern NIC queue identity and binding
claims.claim: Queue effects require live QueueTag, queue epoch, ring, q_vector, NAPI instance, IRQ route, and queue budget.
gaps: No queue epoch emission or check.
No queue budget root below Linux.

claims.title: Typed modern NIC submit classes
claims.claim: SKB, XDP frame, XDP_TX page-pool, and AF_XDP zero-copy submits are distinct authority classes.
gaps: No Domain/epoch correlation on packet memory.
No frozen XDP or AF_XDP descriptor authority.

claims.title: Descriptor publication and doorbell ledger
claims.claim: Descriptor writes, next_to_watch publication, in-flight DMA state, and tail doorbell publication consume a typed DescriptorLedger.
gaps: No stale descriptor quarantine after revoke.

claims.title: DMA packet memory ownership
claims.claim: DMA-capable packet memory requires MemoryView/IOMMU ownership and class-specific memory authority.
gaps: No monitor-backed MemoryView for packet pages.
No IOMMU ownership root below Linux.
No IOMMU invalidation latency or ordering proof.

claims.title: Modern NIC completion settlement
claims.claim: NAPI, IRQ, worker, or service completion settles a previously typed ledger under service authority and service budget.
gaps: No service BudgetTicket or service budget rule.
No proof that completion cannot deliver after revoke.

claims.title: Modern NIC QueueControl authority
claims.claim: Devlink rate/scheduler and VF/SF/representor lifecycle operations require QueueControl authority and a live queue epoch.

claims.title: Modern NIC RepresentorForward authority
claims.claim: Representor transmit requires RepresentorForward authority plus a live lower QueueLease, fresh lower queue epoch, and service budget.
gaps: No fresh lower queue epoch check.

claims.title: Modern NIC service work and async provenance
claims.claim: Reset, PTP, DPLL, eswitch, LAG, DIM, firmware, and maintenance work execute as service/kernel authority classes and cannot create caller-attributed effects without typed carriers.
gaps: No merge policy for coalesced work.
No cancellation/quarantine rule for stale queued work.

claims.title: Modern NIC queue revoke semantics
claims.claim: Queue revoke invalidates submit, descriptor, doorbell, in-flight DMA, completion, control, representor, and service work before authority can be reused or delivered to another Domain.

claims.title: Modern NIC Linux substrate compatibility
claims.claim: Linux netdev, NAPI, XDP, AF_XDP, devlink, representor, workqueue, and tracepoint structures are useful compatibility substrates but not production authority roots.

claims.title: Epoch revoke invalidates all authority paths
claims.claim: Domain, process, object, queue, endpoint, and cluster epoch revocation invalidates active, queued, delayed, async, remote, and cached authority before reuse.

claims.title: Cluster leases compile local before use
claims.claim: Cluster-wide authority compiles into node-local Domain epoch, SchedContext, EndpointCap, BudgetTicket, and QueueLease state before local use.

claims.title: Linux compatibility is preserved
claims.claim: CapSched refines Linux scheduler, cgroup, cpuset, namespace, cred, LSM, rlimit, hotplug, and topology constraints without silently bypassing them or breaking ABI compatibility.

claims.title: Monitor and service domains stay smaller than VM/VMM attack area
claims.claim: The production TCB is small enough to improve or preserve attack surface while reducing cost versus per-VM guest kernels.

claims.title: Co-tenancy and side-channel policy is explicit
claims.claim: CPU core, SMT, cache, NUMA, device, and queue co-tenancy decisions are explicit Domain policy.

claims.title: Exploit and cost baselines are tested
claims.claim: CapSched-H provides VM-like cross-Domain protection and better cost efficiency for selected datacenter workloads.
gaps: No cost, tail-latency, throughput, or density benchmark.

evidence.limitation: Full integration TLC run stopped after state explosion and is not a pass.
Finite model checks authority-class separation; it is not real NIC enforcement evidence.
Finite model checks XDP/AF_XDP memory ownership constraints; it is not real page-pool, XSK, UMEM, or IOMMU enforcement evidence.
Finite model checks QueueControl and RepresentorForward separation; it is not devlink, VF/SF, eswitch, or bridge policy correctness evidence.
Linux source anchors are mutable Linux substrate and not protection evidence.
Every readiness row is observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, and monitor_verified=false.
Finite model checks revoke/drain/quarantine semantic constraints; it is not real queue reset, DMA drain, IRQ masking, IOMMU invalidation, or monitor enforcement evidence.
Maps ice shutdown/rebuild source anchors to formal revoke obligations; it is source-observed only and not protection evidence.
Socket endpoint checks cannot rely only on LSM because some paths reuse sock_sendmsg_nosec().

counterexamples.meaning: Domain switch without flush or retag leaves stale TLB authority.
Per-overlay commit without base serialization permits stale commit.
IRQ aliasing across non-free queues must be forbidden.
Full ClusterLease integration TLC did not finish and is not a pass.

forbidden_claims: task_struct Domain pointers create a hard security boundary.
SchedContext budget is monitor root budget.
Linux page/slab/memcg/page-cache metadata is memory authority.
VFIO/iommufd Linux objects are production queue authority roots.
Linux netdev, ring, q_vector, devlink, representor, tracepoint, or workqueue state is modern NIC QueueLease authority.
SKB, XDP frame, XDP_TX page-pool, AF_XDP zero-copy, QueueControl, RepresentorForward, and ServiceWork can be collapsed into one queue capability.
XDP_TX or AF_XDP zero-copy can borrow SKB authority, generic netdev reachability, or ambient driver worker authority.
QueueControl can be authorized by RunCap, CAP_NET_ADMIN alone, or plain netdev reachability.
Representor netdev reachability is lower QueueLease authority.
Linux netdev down/reset proves monitor-grade queue revoke.
BPF or sched_ext is the production root for No RunCap, no run.
TLC completion alone proves the datacenter OS goal.

gates.title: Vocabulary and build compatibility
gates.allowed_claim: CONFIG_CAPSCHED scaffolding and type-only vocabulary build without changing Linux scheduler behavior.

gates.title: Assurance case foundation
exit_criteria: Next Linux slice must name which claim it supports.

gates.title: Trace-only Linux observation
allowed: Domain shadow identity trace or debug counters.

next_linux_choice: Slice 0C trace-only Domain shadow identity and transition observation.
Narrow source-analysis gate for wakeup/enqueue/runnable-state coverage before any trace patch.
safer_default: Run the source-analysis gate if any scheduler path coverage question remains unclear.
